prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(create_disruption_case primary_passenger_record_1)
(allocate_contact_channel_to_case primary_passenger_record_1 contact_channel_call_centre)
(link_flight_segment_to_case primary_passenger_record_1 flight_segment_1)
(triage_case primary_passenger_record_1 flight_segment_1)
(assign_onsite_staff_to_case primary_passenger_record_1 onsite_staff_agent_1)
(assess_original_option_for_primary_passenger primary_passenger_record_1 original_flight_option_a flight_segment_1)
(confirm_primary_passenger_original_option primary_passenger_record_1 original_flight_option_a onsite_staff_agent_1)
(release_onsite_staff_from_case primary_passenger_record_1 onsite_staff_agent_1)
(release_flight_segment_from_case primary_passenger_record_1 flight_segment_1)
(create_disruption_case co_traveller_record_1)
(allocate_contact_channel_to_case co_traveller_record_1 contact_channel_mobile_app)
(link_flight_segment_to_case co_traveller_record_1 flight_segment_1)
(triage_case co_traveller_record_1 flight_segment_1)
(assign_onsite_staff_to_case co_traveller_record_1 onsite_staff_agent_1)
(assess_alternate_option_for_co_traveller co_traveller_record_1 alternate_flight_option_b flight_segment_1)
(confirm_co_traveller_alternate_option co_traveller_record_1 alternate_flight_option_b onsite_staff_agent_1)
(release_onsite_staff_from_case co_traveller_record_1 onsite_staff_agent_1)
(release_flight_segment_from_case co_traveller_record_1 flight_segment_1)
(assemble_rebooking_proposal_standard primary_passenger_record_1 co_traveller_record_1 original_flight_option_a alternate_flight_option_b rebooking_proposal_slot_1)
(link_flight_segment_to_case primary_passenger_record_1 flight_segment_1)
(select_rebooking_proposal_for_passenger rebooking_proposal_slot_1 primary_passenger_record_1 flight_segment_1)
(release_flight_segment_from_case primary_passenger_record_1 flight_segment_1)
(resolve_primary_passenger_case primary_passenger_record_1 rebooking_proposal_slot_1)
(resolve_co_traveller_case co_traveller_record_1 rebooking_proposal_slot_1)
(create_disruption_case service_case_1)
(allocate_contact_channel_to_case service_case_1 contact_channel_checkin_desk)
(link_flight_segment_to_case service_case_1 flight_segment_1)
(triage_case service_case_1 flight_segment_1)
(attach_supplier_confirmation_to_service_case service_case_1 supplier_confirmation_1)
(attach_external_approval_to_service_case service_case_1 external_approval_1)
(assign_onsite_staff_to_case service_case_1 onsite_staff_agent_1)
(attach_accommodation_option_to_proposal service_case_1 accommodation_option_hotel_1 rebooking_proposal_slot_1)
(validate_rebooking_proposal service_case_1 accommodation_option_hotel_1 rebooking_proposal_slot_1 flight_segment_1)
(record_supplier_confirmation service_case_1 supplier_confirmation_1 onsite_staff_agent_1 accommodation_option_hotel_1 rebooking_proposal_slot_1)
(record_external_approval_and_mark service_case_1 external_approval_1 accommodation_option_hotel_1 rebooking_proposal_slot_1)
(final_approve_service_case service_case_1)
(initiate_compensation_for_case service_case_1 compensation_document_1 flight_segment_1)
(assign_contact_channel_to_service_case service_case_1 contact_channel_checkin_desk compensation_document_1)
(release_flight_segment_from_case service_case_1 flight_segment_1)
(release_supplier_confirmation_from_service_case service_case_1 supplier_confirmation_1)
(release_external_approval_from_service_case service_case_1 external_approval_1)
(create_disruption_case service_case_2)
(allocate_contact_channel_to_case service_case_2 contact_channel_checkin_desk)
(link_flight_segment_to_case service_case_2 flight_segment_1)
(triage_case service_case_2 flight_segment_1)
(attach_supplier_confirmation_to_service_case service_case_2 supplier_confirmation_1)
(attach_external_approval_to_service_case service_case_2 external_approval_1)
(assign_onsite_staff_to_case service_case_2 onsite_staff_agent_1)
(attach_accommodation_option_to_proposal service_case_2 accommodation_option_hotel_2 rebooking_proposal_slot_1)
(validate_rebooking_proposal service_case_2 accommodation_option_hotel_2 rebooking_proposal_slot_1 flight_segment_1)
(record_supplier_confirmation service_case_2 supplier_confirmation_1 onsite_staff_agent_1 accommodation_option_hotel_2 rebooking_proposal_slot_1)
(record_external_approval_and_mark service_case_2 external_approval_1 accommodation_option_hotel_2 rebooking_proposal_slot_1)
(final_approve_service_case service_case_2)
(initiate_compensation_for_case service_case_2 compensation_document_1 flight_segment_1)
(assign_contact_channel_to_service_case service_case_2 contact_channel_checkin_desk compensation_document_1)
(release_flight_segment_from_case service_case_2 flight_segment_1)
(release_supplier_confirmation_from_service_case service_case_2 supplier_confirmation_1)
(release_external_approval_from_service_case service_case_2 external_approval_1)
(link_flight_segment_to_case primary_passenger_record_1 flight_segment_1)
(initiate_compensation_for_case primary_passenger_record_1 compensation_document_1 flight_segment_1)
(assign_contact_channel_to_primary_passenger primary_passenger_record_1 contact_channel_call_centre compensation_document_1)
(release_flight_segment_from_case primary_passenger_record_1 flight_segment_1)
(link_flight_segment_to_case co_traveller_record_1 flight_segment_1)
(initiate_compensation_for_case co_traveller_record_1 compensation_document_1 flight_segment_1)
(assign_contact_channel_to_co_traveller co_traveller_record_1 contact_channel_mobile_app compensation_document_1)
```

message.reasoning:
The goal is to achieve contact_channel_confirmed for primary_passenger_record_1, co_traveller_record_1, service_case_1, and service_case_2.


## Investigating Contact Channel Confirmation

- The goal is to ensure contact_channel_confirmed status for primary_passenger_record_1, co_traveller_record_1, service_case_1, and service_case_2.  
- This status likely stems from specific actions taken to confirm the contact channel.

## Investigating Contact Channel Confirmation

- The goal requires contact_channel_confirmed for primary_passenger_record_1, co_traveller_record_1, service_case_1, and service_case_2, linked to the same passengers and proposal.  
- Both service cases are connected to third-party partners, but third_party_approval_partner_2 is initially unavailable, limiting options.  

## Checking Case Resolution Process

- To achieve contact_channel_confirmed, each case must go through case_resolution_ready and compensation_initiated, involving actions like resolve_primary_passenger_case or close_service_case_after_external_approval.  
- The process includes steps like triage, staff assignment, and inventory allocation, which may complicate achieving the goal for all four cases.  

## Exploring Case Creation

- None of the cases are created yet, so first step is create_disruption_case for each case_subject, setting case_created as a precondition.  
- After creation, allocate_contact_channel_to_case requires contact_channel_available, but confirmation likely needs the full resolution process.  

## Assessing Resource Availability

- Only three contact channels are available, which may limit processing all four cases simultaneously.  
- The availability of third_party_approval_partner_1 but not partner_2 adds complexity to service_case_2 resolution.

## Mapping Case Resolution Steps

- For primary_passenger_record_1, the process starts with allocate_contact_channel_to_case, then links to flight_segment_1, followed by triage and onsite staff assignment.  
- Assessing the original option requires the case to be triaged, linked to a flight, and not yet assessed, leading to original_option_assessed status.  

## Examining Option Confirmation

- Confirming the original option for primary_passenger_record_1 needs the case triaged, onsite staff assigned, and the option assessed, resulting in primary_assessed and primary_option_confirmed.  
- The process avoids inventory allocation, using assess-then-confirm with onsite staff, fitting the standard path since primary_passenger_original_option_link exists.  

## Extending to Co-Travellers

- For co_traveller_record_1, assessing alternate options follows a similar path: requires triaging, flight links, and an existing option link, leading to assessed status.  
- Confirming the alternate option for co-travellers also requires triaging, onsite assignment, and assessment, ensuring co_assessed and co_option_confirmed.  

## Assembling Rebooking Proposals

- Assembling a standard rebooking proposal requires both primary and co-traveller options assessed and confirmed, with no allocation recorded, reserving the proposal.  
- The proposal includes both passengers, fitting the standard assemble path since confirmation was done without inventory allocation.  

## Finalizing Case Selection

- Selecting the rebooking proposal is specific to the primary passenger, requiring it to be reserved and the primary assessed, with flight links confirmed.  
- Resolving both primary and co-traveller cases needs the proposal selected (from the primary's selection), assessed, confirmed, and reserved, leading to resolution_ready.

## Evaluating Service Case Options

- For service_case_2, third_party_approval_partner_2 is unavailable, limiting the use of third-party approval paths.
- Alternative approaches, like using a shared proposal path with passengers, may be viable since service_case_links_proposal exists and rebooking_proposal_selected is confirmed.

## Handling Accommodation Attachments

- Attaching accommodation options to a proposal requires the case triaged, service linked to accommodation, and the proposal reserved and selected, without allocation.
- The effect is allocated, assigned to the proposal, and no longer available, enabling further validation steps.

## Validating Rebooking Proposals

- Validating a rebooking proposal needs case_triaged, service linked to accommodation, allocated, and assigned, with flight links confirmed, and no supplier confirmation required.
- The validation process results in proposal_validated, skipping supplier confirmation if not required, streamlining the process.

## Recording External Approvals

- Recording external approvals and marking readiness requires supplier_confirmed, links to external and accommodation, and accommodation tied to the proposal, without supplier or third-party requirements.
- This path fits standard proposals, leading to ready_final status, ensuring closure without additional confirmations.

## Confirming Proposal Validation

- Proposal validation requires case linked to flight, service linked to accommodation, allocated, and assigned, with no supplier requirement, currently satisfied.
- Validation process results in proposal_validated, enabling further steps like recording supplier confirmation.

## Recording Supplier Confirmation

- Recording supplier confirmation needs proposal_validated, supplier linked, onsite staff assigned, and accommodation tied to the proposal, with no third-party requirement.
- This action sets supplier_confirmed, preparing for external approval recording.

## Marking External Approvals

- Recording external approvals requires supplier_confirmed, links to external and accommodation, and no supplier or third-party requirements, fitting standard proposals.
- The effect is ready_for_finalization, setting the stage for final case approval.

## Finalizing Service Case

- Final approving the service case requires ready_for_finalization, not ready_for_compensation_selection, and not closed, currently met.
- The action closes the case and marks it as case_resolution_ready, allowing resource release if needed.

## Managing Resource Release

- Releasing onsite staff from the case is possible after case resolution, but accommodation release actions aren’t specified.
- Next steps involve checking resource reuse for service_case_2, with contact channels already allocated but not confirmed yet.

## Releasing Resources

- After confirmation, resources like flights, suppliers, and external approvals can be released manually for reuse, especially for service_case_2.
- Staff release occurs after final approval, ensuring resources are available for new cases without conflicts.

## Handling Proposal Attachments

- Attaching accommodations to proposals requires the case to be triaged and linked to a proposal, ensuring all prerequisites like availability and selection are met.
- This process doesn’t require supplier confirmation, fitting cases where documentation isn’t needed.

## Managing Passenger Processing

- Early allocation of case channels for triage ensures smooth passenger processing, remaining allocated until case resolution.
- Proposals selected by primary options confirm links to flight segments, supporting resolution without additional requirements.

## Managing Service Case 2

- Created a new disruption case for service_case_2, linking flight_segment_1 and allocating contact channels like check-in desk.
- Attached supplier confirmation, external approvals, and accommodations to proposals, ensuring all prerequisites like triage and staff assignment are met.

## Processing Compensation

- Initiated compensation for service_case_2 and passenger records, linking flight segments and assigning contact channels like call centers or mobile apps.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
